decks.notes: The deck is focused on using the card Doomsday to set up a winning combo using a combination of Thassa's Oracle and The Fantasticar. By manipulating the top of the deck with cards like Brainstorm and Ponder, the player can stack the deck to ensure a successful Doomsday resolution. This combo allows for a quick and powerful win condition, while also utilizing disruptive spells like Force of Will and Daze to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent's strategy.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize powerful reanimation spells like Reanimate and Animate Dead to cheat out expensive or game-changing creatures like Griselbrand and Archon of Cruelty. The deck also includes cards like Faithless Looting, Collective Brutality, and Thoughtseize to help set up the combo and disrupt the opponent's game plan. With cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal for fast mana acceleration, this deck aims to quickly put big threats onto the battlefield and overwhelm the opponent before they can respond effectively.

This deck aims to disrupt opponents with counterspells like Force of Will and Daze while applying early pressure with efficient creatures like Orcish Bowmasters and Murktide Regent. It also utilizes hand disruption with Thoughtseize and removal like Fatal Push to control the board. The deck's tempo-focused strategy is further supported by cards like Brazen Borrower and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student, allowing for quick swings in momentum and maintaining control of the game. Its land destruction elements with Wasteland also help to disrupt opponents' mana base and slow down their game plan.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Archon of Cruelty, then use reanimation spells such as Reanimate and Animate Dead to bring them back onto the battlefield. Cards like Unmask and Thoughtseize help to disrupt the opponent's game plan while Faithless Looting and Collective Brutality provide card advantage and flexibility. The deck aims to win the game through powerful combo turns using creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory.

The main idea of this deck is to control the game through a combination of lands and spells. Using cards like Thespian's Stage, Dark Depths, and Crop Rotation, the deck aims to generate powerful land synergies. Alongside this, cards like Wasteland and Mox Diamond provide disruption and acceleration. The inclusion of control elements like Pithing Needle and Grafdigger's Cage help to disrupt opponents' strategies. Overall, the deck seeks to establish a strong board presence while controlling the game through a combination of land interactions and disruptive spells.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by utilizing Nic Fit archetype cards and various removal spells to disrupt the opponent's strategy while generating incremental value through sacrificing creatures and lands. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent by recurring threats, disrupting their hand with Cabal Therapy, and using cards like Malevolent Rumble and Pernicious Deed to wipe the board when necessary. It also includes graveyard recursion and utility lands to ensure that the player has the resources needed to stay ahead in the game.

The deck revolves around cheating in powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier onto the battlefield quickly using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. The strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with these massive threats before they have a chance to respond, while also using counterspells like Force of Will to protect the combo. The deck also includes ramp like Lotus Petal and fast lands like Ancient Tomb to accelerate the game plan, with some cantrips to help find key pieces quickly.

The deck focuses on locking down opponents with artifact-based control elements, using cards like Trinisphere and Karn, the Great Creator to restrict their options. It also aims to ramp up quickly with Urza lands and Grim Monolith to quickly deploy powerful threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn. The inclusion of cards like Candelabra of Tawnos and Voltaic Key allows for additional synergies and flexibility in manipulating mana sources. Overall, the deck's strategy revolves around establishing a board presence while disrupting the opponent's game plan with a variety of artifacts and powerful spells.

This deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo involving Mystic Forge and various artifacts to generate mana, draw cards, and ultimately win the game. By utilizing cards like The One Ring, Grim Monolith, and Glaring Fleshraker, the deck aims to quickly ramp up to its powerful Mystic Forge engine and overwhelm the opponent. With support from Tezzeret, Cruel Captain and Urza's Saga, the deck has the ability to continuously generate value and maintain a strong board presence. The goal is to outpace and outmaneuver the opponent through efficient use of artifacts and combo pieces.

The key concept of this deck is to generate a high storm count by casting multiple spells in a single turn using fast mana sources like Lion's Eye Diamond, Lotus Petal, and Dark Ritual. By chaining together spells such as Burning Wish and Beseech the Mirror, the deck aims to find and cast powerful finishers like Tendrils of Agony or Runehorn Hellkite to win the game. Echo of Eons can be used to refill the hand and continue the storm combo. The deck also utilizes cards like Chrome Mox and Mox Opal to accelerate the mana and enable explosive plays.

The main idea of this deck is to control the game through various spells and creatures, utilizing cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck also aims to generate card advantage with cards like Baleful Strix and Uro, Titan of Nature's Wrath while utilizing powerful removal spells like Fatal Push and Sheoldred's Edict to keep the opponent's board in check. The inclusion of Life from the Loam and Wasteland allows for additional resource denial, while Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Lórien Revealed provide card draw and selection to fuel the control game plan.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and efficiently get Arclight Phoenix into play and start attacking the opponent. The deck uses a combination of cheap spells like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal to power out spells like Buried Alive and Cabal Ritual to get the Phoenix into the graveyard, then uses cards like Flow State and The Fantasticar to bring the Phoenix back from the graveyard and onto the battlefield. The deck also includes disruption spells like Duress and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on using powerful artifacts and control elements to create a prison-like effect, shutting down opponent's strategies while setting up your own win condition. Key cards like Karn, the Great Creator, Trinisphere, and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms are used to disrupt opponent's game plan, while cards like Grim Monolith, Urza's Saga, and Urza's Tower generate mana and provide resources for powerful plays. The ultimate goal is to lock down the opponent's options and overwhelm them with powerful artifacts like The One Ring and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn.

The main idea of this Eldrazi Aggro deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Chalice of the Void and Thought-Knot Seer while deploying efficient threats like Reality Smasher and Sire of Seven Deaths. By utilizing cards that generate mana like Lotus Petal and Ancient Tomb, the deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with large Eldrazi creatures. The combination of disruption and aggression makes this deck a formidable force in the Legacy format, especially when backed up by powerful lands like Eye of Ugin and Cavern of Souls.

This combo deck focuses on using Show and Tell to cheat powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn into play for free. It also utilizes Omniscience to cast spells for free, allowing for explosive turns where multiple threats can be played at once. Cunning Wish and Intuition provide additional flexibility and toolbox options. Force of Will and Spell Pierce help protect the combo from disruption, while Veil of Summer provides protection against blue disruption spells. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with massive creatures and game-winning spells.

The main concept of the deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Archon of Cruelty, then bring them back into play using reanimation spells like Reanimate and Animate Dead. This combo-oriented strategy aims to overwhelm the opponent with early threats and disruption through cards like Cabal Therapy and Thoughtseize, while also utilizing cards like Faithless Looting to dig for key pieces. The deck operates on a fast and aggressive game plan, aiming to close out games quickly before the opponent can establish their own strategy.
